common_fields.label: Brand Name
common_fields.description: Brand, client brand, product line, or working brand associated with the concept.
common_fields.capture_intent: Understand whether the concept should be anchored to an existing brand or generated more neutrally.
used_for_generation: Align tone and framing with the brand context
Make concepts feel branded rather than generic when relevant
common_fields.fallback_behavior: Treat as neutral or unbranded concept generation.
sample_questions: Which brand or product line is this concept for?
Should I create this for a specific brand, or keep it brand-neutral?

common_fields.label: Category
common_fields.description: The category or domain in which the concept should be created.
common_fields.capture_intent: Anchor the concept in the correct market and product space.
used_for_generation: Ensure relevant language, use cases, and category logic
common_fields.fallback_behavior: Must be inferred from context or clarified before meaningful concept generation.
sample_questions: What category is this concept for?
Which product or service space should I generate ideas in?

common_fields.label: Market
common_fields.description: Country, region, or market where the concept will be explored or tested.
sample_questions: Which market or country should this concept be created for?
Is this intended for a specific region or audience market?

common_fields.label: Research Objective
common_fields.description: What the researcher wants to learn, compare, optimize, or test through these concepts.
common_fields.capture_intent: Understand why the concepts are being generated.
used_for_generation: Shape the breadth and style of concepts
Optimize for testing readiness versus early exploration
sample_questions: What are you hoping to learn or test with these concepts?
Is the goal idea exploration, concept testing, or refinement?

common_fields.label: Business Objective
common_fields.description: Commercial or strategic goal behind the concept generation.
common_fields.capture_intent: Understand what business direction the concept should support.
used_for_generation: Bias concept direction toward the desired strategic goal
sample_questions: What business goal should these concepts support?
Should the concepts help with premiumization, differentiation, trial, or something else?

common_fields.label: Target Audience
common_fields.description: Who the concept is for, including demographic, behavioral, attitudinal, or need-state detail.
common_fields.capture_intent: Identify who should find the concept relevant.
used_for_generation: Tailor the problem, tone, and benefit framing
sample_questions: Who is the concept meant for?
Can you describe the target consumer or user group?

common_fields.label: Consumer Need or Tension
common_fields.description: Underlying pain point, unmet need, desire, or friction that the concept should address.
common_fields.capture_intent: Understand the human problem or motivation behind the concept.
used_for_generation: Build concepts around a meaningful consumer insight
common_fields.fallback_behavior: Infer broad category-level needs if possible.
sample_questions: What consumer problem or need should this concept address?
What tension, frustration, or desire is most important here?

common_fields.label: Must Include
common_fields.description: Words, features, claims, messages, assets, ingredients, or cues that must appear.
used_for_generation: Make outputs more usable for downstream research
sample_questions: Are there any things that must be included in the concepts?
Do you have any mandatory words, features, claims, or cues I should use?

common_fields.label: Must Avoid
common_fields.description: Words, territories, tones, visuals, claims, or ideas that should not appear.
common_fields.capture_intent: Set exclusions and brand or legal boundaries.
used_for_generation: Avoid risky, repetitive, or brand-inappropriate directions
sample_questions: Is there anything I should avoid in the concepts?
Any words, tones, territories, or ideas that should not be used?

common_fields.label: Reference Inputs
common_fields.description: Existing concepts, packs, ads, claims, visuals, or competitor references provided by the user.
common_fields.capture_intent: Ground concept generation in prior materials or reference territory.
used_for_generation: Use existing material as inspiration or grounding
Avoid duplicating prior directions when needed
sample_questions: Do you have any reference concepts, claims, packs, or examples I should build from?
Should I take inspiration from any existing material or competitor examples?

common_fields.label: Output Preferences
common_fields.description: Preferences for number of concepts, structure, tone, length, or formatting.
common_fields.capture_intent: Understand how the output should be packaged.
sample_questions: How would you like the concepts to be presented?
Do you want short concept options, detailed writeups, or something in between?

product_concept.purpose: Generate a full product or service concept.
product_concept.minimum_generation_rule: Need category and at least one of target audience, consumer need or tension, main benefit, or product format.

fields.label: Product Format
fields.description: What the product or service actually is, such as beverage, snack bar, app feature bundle, serum, or subscription plan.
fields.capture_intent: Define the concrete offer form.
fields.fallback_behavior: Infer likely format from the category.
sample_questions: What kind of product or service should this concept be?
Do you already know the format, such as a drink, snack, app, or service?

fields.label: Problem to Solve
fields.description: The specific consumer problem or pain point the product or service should solve.
fields.capture_intent: Clarify the main problem the concept addresses.
sample_questions: What problem should this product solve?
What is frustrating or missing for consumers today?

fields.label: Main Benefit
fields.description: The primary benefit or promise of the concept.
fields.capture_intent: Define the central value proposition.
fields.fallback_behavior: Infer from problem or category context.
sample_questions: What is the main benefit this concept should deliver?
If the consumer remembers one promise, what should it be?

fields.label: Secondary Benefits
fields.description: Additional supporting benefits that complement the main promise.
fields.capture_intent: Capture extra value layers around the concept.
fields.fallback_behavior: Keep the concept focused on the main benefit only.
sample_questions: Are there any secondary benefits you also want included?
Beyond the main benefit, what else should this concept deliver?

fields.label: Reason to Believe
fields.description: Proof points, ingredients, features, mechanisms, or evidence that make the concept believable.
fields.capture_intent: Understand what makes the promise credible.
fields.fallback_behavior: Use softer support language without strong proof claims.
sample_questions: Why should consumers believe this concept?
Are there any ingredients, features, or proof points that support the promise?

fields.label: Usage Occasion
fields.description: When, where, or in what situation the concept would be used.
fields.capture_intent: Understand the usage context and need-state.
used_for_generation: Make the concept more vivid and situational
sample_questions: When or where would people use this concept?
Is there a specific occasion or moment this should be designed for?

fields.label: Differentiation
fields.description: What should make the concept stand out from existing alternatives.
fields.capture_intent: Identify the uniqueness of the concept.
fields.fallback_behavior: Differentiate using tone, audience, or benefit mix.
sample_questions: What should make this concept feel different from what already exists?
Where should the uniqueness of this concept come from?

fields.label: Price Positioning
fields.description: Whether the concept should feel premium, mainstream, affordable, or luxury.
fields.capture_intent: Understand the intended value tier.
sample_questions: Should this concept feel premium, mainstream, or value-led?
What kind of price or value positioning should I assume?

feature_innovation_concept.purpose: Generate a feature, capability, ingredient, or innovation concept.
feature_innovation_concept.minimum_generation_rule: Need category or parent product and at least one of innovation focus, functional value, or current consumer pain.

fields.label: Parent Product or Brand
fields.description: The existing product, service, or brand this innovation belongs to.
fields.capture_intent: Understand the base offer the innovation builds on.
used_for_generation: Keep the innovation connected to the base offer
Maintain continuity with current brand or product logic
sample_questions: Is this innovation tied to an existing product or brand?
What current product or offer should this new feature build on?

fields.label: Innovation Focus
fields.description: What is actually new, such as a feature, ingredient, technology, workflow, mechanism, or enhancement.
fields.capture_intent: Define the specific innovation at the center of the concept.
used_for_generation: Anchor the concept in the new element
Prevent the concept from becoming too broad
fields.fallback_behavior: Infer from context if possible, otherwise clarify.
sample_questions: What is the actual innovation you want to explore?
What is new or different in this feature or idea?

fields.label: Current Consumer Pain
fields.description: The problem in the current experience that the innovation improves.
fields.capture_intent: Understand why the innovation matters.
sample_questions: What problem with the current experience should this innovation solve?
What is not working well enough today that this new feature should improve?

fields.label: Functional Value
fields.description: The practical or performance benefit delivered by the innovation.
fields.capture_intent: Define the concrete improvement created by the innovation.
used_for_generation: Keep the concept easy to understand
sample_questions: What practical improvement should this innovation deliver?
What does this feature do better for the user?

fields.label: Emotional Value
fields.description: How the innovation should make the user feel.
fields.capture_intent: Capture the emotional upside beyond utility.
used_for_generation: Add resonance beyond the functional story
fields.fallback_behavior: Focus on the functional story only.
sample_questions: How should this innovation make the user feel?
Is there an emotional payoff you want this feature to create?

fields.label: Proof or Mechanism
fields.description: How the innovation works, including science, technology, design logic, or operating mechanism.
fields.capture_intent: Make the innovation more credible and explainable.
sample_questions: How does this innovation actually work?
Is there a mechanism, technology, or logic behind it that I should reflect?

fields.label: Fit with Existing Brand
fields.description: Whether the innovation should feel fully on-brand, adjacent, or more disruptive.
fields.capture_intent: Understand the desired level of stretch versus familiarity.
used_for_generation: Adjust how radical the concept feels
fields.fallback_behavior: Assume moderate fit with the existing brand.
sample_questions: Should this innovation feel fully on-brand or more disruptive?
How close to the current brand or product should this new idea feel?

fields.label: Barrier or Confusion to Address
fields.description: Any skepticism, misunderstanding, or friction the concept should proactively address.
fields.capture_intent: Identify barriers that may limit adoption or understanding.
used_for_generation: Make the concept easier to understand and accept
fields.fallback_behavior: Do not explicitly address barriers unless inferred.
sample_questions: Is there any skepticism or confusion this concept should address?
What might stop people from immediately understanding or believing this innovation?

claim_concept.purpose: Generate or refine benefit, proof, or communication claims.
claim_concept.minimum_generation_rule: Need category or product context and at least one of benefit to communicate or claim goal.

fields.label: Claim Goal
fields.description: What the claim should achieve, such as attention, reassurance, premiumization, simplification, or differentiation.
fields.capture_intent: Understand the job the claim needs to do.
used_for_generation: Set the strategic direction of the claim
fields.fallback_behavior: Default to clear and persuasive wording.
sample_questions: What should the claim achieve?
Should the claim grab attention, build trust, simplify the message, or do something else?

fields.label: Product or Brand Context
fields.description: The product, service, or brand the claim belongs to.
fields.capture_intent: Anchor the claim in the correct context.
sample_questions: What product or brand is this claim for?
Which offer or variant should the claim be tied to?

fields.label: Benefit to Communicate
fields.description: The core benefit or promise the claim should express.
fields.capture_intent: Define the central message of the claim.
fields.fallback_behavior: Infer from product context if possible.
sample_questions: What benefit should the claim communicate?
What is the main promise or outcome the claim needs to express?

fields.label: Claim Type
fields.description: Functional, emotional, scientific, natural, superiority, convenience, value, or sustainability claim type.
fields.capture_intent: Define the territory and framing style of the claim.
fields.fallback_behavior: Use a neutral benefit-led claim style.
sample_questions: What kind of claim are you looking for?
Should the claim feel more functional, emotional, scientific, natural, or something else?

fields.label: Substantiation Level
fields.description: How strongly the benefit is supported, such as strong proof, partial proof, soft support, or exploratory.
fields.capture_intent: Control how aggressive or cautious the wording should be.
sample_questions: How strongly is this claim supported?
Do you have strong proof behind this claim, partial support, or is it still exploratory?

fields.label: Mandatory Words
fields.description: Words or phrases that must appear in the claim output.
fields.capture_intent: Capture required lexical constraints.
sample_questions: Are there any words or phrases that must appear in the claim?
Do you have any required wording I should include?

fields.label: Prohibited Words
fields.description: Words or phrases that should not appear in the claim output.
fields.capture_intent: Capture wording exclusions and risk boundaries.
sample_questions: Are there any words I should avoid using in the claim?
Do you want me to avoid any specific terms, tones, or phrases?

fields.label: Claim Tone
fields.description: Tone such as clinical, premium, simple, emotional, assertive, soft, or modern.
fields.capture_intent: Define the stylistic tone of the claim.
sample_questions: What tone should the claim have?
Should the claim feel clinical, premium, simple, emotional, or something else?

fields.label: Variants Needed
fields.description: How many claim options or variants should be generated.
fields.capture_intent: Set breadth of output.
used_for_generation: Determine how many options to generate
sample_questions: How many claim options would you like?
Do you want just a few focused claims or a broader set of variants?

visual_image_pack_concept.purpose: Generate visual, image, key visual, or pack direction concepts.
visual_image_pack_concept.minimum_generation_rule: Need category or asset type and at least one of message to visualize, visual mood, or brand context.

fields.label: Asset Type
fields.description: The visual output type, such as image, pack, front-of-pack, hero shot, key visual, usage visual, or moodboard.
fields.capture_intent: Define the exact visual asset being conceptualized.
used_for_generation: Separate pack logic from scene logic
fields.fallback_behavior: Default to a general visual direction.
sample_questions: What kind of visual output do you want to create?
Is this for an image, a pack direction, a key visual, or something else?

fields.label: Message to Visualize
fields.description: What the visual should communicate immediately or make people feel.
fields.capture_intent: Define the communication task of the visual.
sample_questions: What should the visual communicate right away?
What is the key message or impression the image or pack should convey?

fields.label: Visual Mood
fields.description: The desired emotional feel, such as energetic, calming, premium, playful, indulgent, natural, or clinical.
fields.capture_intent: Define the emotional feel of the visual concept.
sample_questions: What mood should the visual create?
Should the visual feel energetic, premium, calming, playful, or something else?

fields.label: Visual Style
fields.description: Style direction such as realistic, illustrative, minimal, lifestyle, bold, premium, or modern.
fields.capture_intent: Define how the visual should look stylistically.
used_for_generation: Guide visual language and art direction
sample_questions: What kind of visual style do you want?
Should this feel realistic, minimal, bold, illustrative, lifestyle-led, or something else?

fields.label: People Presence
fields.description: Whether people should appear in the visual, what kind of people, or whether it should be product-only.
fields.capture_intent: Control the role of people in the visual.
sample_questions: Should people appear in the visual?
Do you want this to be product-only, or include people and usage context?

fields.label: Key Visual Elements
fields.description: Objects, scenes, ingredients, icons, colors, pack elements, or cues that should appear.
fields.capture_intent: Capture the visual building blocks that matter most.
sample_questions: What key visual elements should be included?
Are there any ingredients, objects, scenes, or pack cues you want featured?

fields.label: Visual Exclusions
fields.description: Objects, colors, scenes, styles, or cues that should not appear.
fields.capture_intent: Capture visual exclusions and boundaries.
sample_questions: Is there anything I should avoid visually?
Any colors, scenes, elements, or styles that should not appear?

fields.label: Brand Visual Cues
fields.description: Colors, design codes, shapes, typography feel, architecture, or assets associated with the brand.
fields.capture_intent: Keep the visual concept aligned with brand identity when needed.
fields.fallback_behavior: Use broad market-fit cues rather than explicit brand codes.
sample_questions: Are there any brand visual cues I should reflect?
Do you have colors, design codes, or pack cues that should guide the concept?

fields.label: Realism Level
fields.description: Whether the visual should feel highly realistic, semi-aspirational, or idealized.
fields.capture_intent: Control realism versus aspiration in the concept.
used_for_generation: Set the level of polish and fantasy
sample_questions: Should the visual feel very realistic or more aspirational?
Do you want this concept grounded in reality or more idealized?

ad_communication_concept.purpose: Generate ad routes, communication concepts, campaign territories, or creative platforms.
ad_communication_concept.minimum_generation_rule: Need category or audience context and at least one of communication task, single-minded message, or main benefit.

fields.label: Channel or Context
fields.description: The communications environment, such as TV, digital video, social, ecommerce, in-store, CRM, or website.
fields.capture_intent: Understand where the communication will live.
sample_questions: Where will this communication concept be used?
Is this for TV, social, digital, ecommerce, in-store, or something else?

fields.label: Communication Task
fields.description: What the communication should do, such as build awareness, explain innovation, create desire, or address skepticism.
fields.capture_intent: Define the job of the communication.
sample_questions: What should the communication achieve?
Is the goal to drive awareness, explain something new, build desire, or overcome skepticism?

fields.label: Single-Minded Message
fields.description: The one thing the audience should remember after seeing the communication.
fields.capture_intent: Define the key takeaway.
sample_questions: What is the one thing people should remember?
If the audience takes away only one message, what should it be?

fields.label: Supporting Messages
fields.description: Secondary messages that support the main communication idea.
fields.capture_intent: Capture message hierarchy beyond the main takeaway.
fields.fallback_behavior: Keep the route tightly focused on one message.
sample_questions: Are there any supporting messages that should sit under the main idea?
What else should the communication mention besides the core takeaway?

fields.label: Barrier to Overcome
fields.description: An objection, misunderstanding, inertia, or skepticism the communication should address.
fields.capture_intent: Identify what is getting in the way of persuasion.
sample_questions: Is there any barrier or objection this communication should tackle?
What might stop people from responding positively to this message?

fields.label: Desired Emotional Response
fields.description: The feeling the communication should evoke, such as trust, excitement, confidence, relief, craving, or pride.
fields.capture_intent: Define the intended emotional effect of the communication.
fields.fallback_behavior: Use a broadly positive emotional tone.
sample_questions: How should the audience feel after seeing this communication?
What emotional response are you aiming for, such as trust, excitement, confidence, or something else?

fields.label: Call to Action or Behavior
fields.description: The desired next step or behavior, such as learn more, buy, try, switch, subscribe, or reconsider.
fields.capture_intent: Identify the intended behavioral outcome.
sample_questions: What action should the audience take next?
Do you want this communication to drive trial, purchase, learning, switching, or something else?

fields.label: Mandatories
fields.description: Required inclusions such as taglines, legal lines, product shots, assets, offers, or spokespersons.
fields.capture_intent: Capture mandatory elements for the communication route.
used_for_generation: Keep the outputs usable and execution-aware
sample_questions: Are there any mandatory elements the communication must include?
Do you have any required taglines, assets, offers, or legal lines I should account for?

value_proposition_naming_concept.purpose: Generate value propositions, offer framing, names, taglines, or message platforms.
value_proposition_naming_concept.minimum_generation_rule: Need output type and at least one of core value proposition, entity to name, or target perception.

fields.label: Output Needed
fields.description: Type of verbal asset needed, such as value proposition, offer framing, product name, service name, tagline, or message platform.
fields.capture_intent: Define what kind of output should be generated.
used_for_generation: Prevent mixing different verbal tasks unintentionally
fields.fallback_behavior: Default to value proposition plus naming ideas.
sample_questions: What kind of output do you need here?
Are you looking for a value proposition, a name, a tagline, or something else?

fields.label: Entity to Name or Frame
fields.description: What exactly is being named or framed, such as product, service, bundle, feature, platform, or line.
fields.capture_intent: Anchor the naming or framing task to the right object.
used_for_generation: Keep the outputs relevant and specific
Avoid abstract naming without a target entity
sample_questions: What exactly needs to be named or framed?
Is this for a product, service, feature, bundle, or something else?

fields.label: Core Value Proposition
fields.description: What the offer fundamentally gives the user and why it is worth choosing.
fields.capture_intent: Define the underlying meaning the naming or proposition should express.
used_for_generation: Create propositions and names with substance
Prevent empty or purely stylistic outputs
fields.fallback_behavior: Infer broad value from category and audience context.
sample_questions: What is the core value this offer provides?
Why should people choose this over other options?

fields.label: Target Perception
fields.description: How the offer or name should be perceived, such as premium, expert, modern, accessible, trustworthy, natural, or bold.
fields.capture_intent: Define the intended impression created by the proposition or name.
used_for_generation: Shape how polished, simple, or premium outputs feel
fields.fallback_behavior: Use clear and broadly positive perception.
sample_questions: How should this name or proposition feel to people?
Should it come across as premium, modern, trustworthy, accessible, or something else?

fields.label: Value Signal
fields.description: The value dimension to emphasize, such as affordability, expertise, speed, convenience, quality, or performance.
fields.capture_intent: Understand which aspect of value should be highlighted most strongly.
sample_questions: What kind of value should this signal most strongly?
Should this feel more about affordability, convenience, quality, expertise, speed, or something else?

fields.label: Naming Style
fields.description: Preferred naming approach such as descriptive, evocative, simple, coined, technical, metaphorical, or benefit-led.
fields.capture_intent: Guide the naming architecture and creative style.
sample_questions: What naming style do you want me to explore?
Should the names feel descriptive, evocative, coined, simple, or something else?

fields.label: Must Include Words
fields.description: Words, stems, semantic cues, or themes that should appear in the output.
fields.capture_intent: Apply lexical constraints to naming or proposition writing.
sample_questions: Are there any words or themes that must be included?
Do you want certain words, stems, or cues reflected in the naming or proposition?

fields.label: Must Avoid Words
fields.description: Words, stems, or semantic territories that should be excluded.
fields.capture_intent: Avoid unwanted lexical or semantic directions.
sample_questions: Are there any words or themes I should avoid?
Do you want me to stay away from any naming territories or expressions?

fields.label: Architecture Constraints
fields.description: Structural rules such as masterbrand endorsement, family naming logic, line extension rules, or character limits.
fields.capture_intent: Keep naming or framing outputs usable within brand architecture.
sample_questions: Are there any naming system rules or structural constraints I should follow?
Should the output fit within an existing brand architecture or naming pattern?
